response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and systems developer with a broad skill set, but he is a poor match for this role across nearly every required dimension. The most immediate disqualifier is the complete absence of Japanese language proficiency, which is listed as a hard requirement. Beyond that, his background is overwhelmingly web and application-layer development (TypeScript, Node.js, Ember.js, MongoDB), with only a brief and peripheral exposure to C/C++ at National Instruments. There is no demonstrated experience in embedded firmware, LAN-switch products, Linux kernel modification, or any of the required security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, encryption). His career trajectory does not intersect with embedded networking or network security engineering in any substantive way.
strengths: Long tenure and breadth of experience across web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded contexts demonstrates adaptability
C/C++ exposure at National Instruments and Linux-based bachelor thesis project shows at least a baseline familiarity with lower-level systems
Strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, pairing) indicate engineering discipline that transfers across domains
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency whatsoever — this is a non-negotiable hard requirement and alone eliminates the candidate
No experience with embedded networking, LAN-switch products, Linux kernel development, or any of the listed security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, encryption, attack detection)
Primary technical stack is web/application-layer (TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB); C programming experience is minimal and dated
